agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement the Order Fulfillment state management feature. Bird has defined the domain invariants for order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"An order cannot transition from SHIPPED to PENDING","testable_assertion":"Calling transitionState(SHIPPED, PENDING) on OrderStateMachine must throw/return ORDER_STATE_INVALID"},{"rule":"A refund can only be issued on a DELIVERED order","testable_assertion":"RefundService.issueRefund(orderId) must reject with an error when order state is anything other than DELIVERED"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose PATCH /orders/{id}/state for state transitions","implementation_note":"The endpoint accepts a target state in the request body. Delegate all transition validation to OrderStateMachine before persisting. Return 409 Conflict with error code on invalid transitions."},{"decision":"OrderStateMachine class owns all transition validation logic","implementation_note":"Encapsulate the full set of allowed state transitions (e.g. PENDING->SHIPPED->DELIVERED). This is the single source of truth for what moves are legal. No transition logic should exist outside this class."},{"decision":"RefundService is a separate service that calls the order service to verify state","implementation_note":"RefundService must query current order state (via the order service or repository) and confirm state == DELIVERED before issuing a refund. Do not duplicate state-checking logic — delegate to OrderStateMachine or read the canonical order state."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Invalid state transition is rejected","given":"An order in SHIPPED state","when":"A revert-to-pending (transition to PENDING) is attempted via PATCH /orders/{id}/state","then":"The request is rejected with error code ORDER_STATE_INVALID and HTTP 409"},{"criterion":"Refund on non-DELIVERED order is rejected","given":"An order in any state other than DELIVERED","when":"A refund is requested via RefundService","then":"The refund is rejected with an appropriate error indicating the order is not in DELIVERED state"},{"criterion":"Refund on DELIVERED order succeeds","given":"An order in DELIVERED state","when":"A refund is requested via RefundService","then":"The refund is issued successfully"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Order state invariant","technical_term":"State transition guard in OrderStateMachine","definition":"A business rule that prohibits certain state transitions. Enforced as guard clauses or a transition whitelist inside OrderStateMachine."},{"domain_term":"Bounded context: Order Fulfillment","technical_term":"Order service boundary","definition":"All order state and refund logic lives within this service boundary. External consumers interact only through the PATCH endpoint and RefundService API."}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":92,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are clearly defined by Bird with testable invariants","Architecture boundaries are well-defined by MJ with clear separation of concerns","No contradictions between Bird's domain analysis and MJ's architecture"],"low_confidence_areas":["Full set of valid state transitions beyond SHIPPED->PENDING not specified — only the prohibited transition is called out","Error handling contract for RefundService (HTTP codes, error body shape) not fully specified"],"assumptions":["HTTP 409 Conflict is the appropriate status code for invalid state transitions","OrderStateMachine will be a pure domain class with no infrastructure dependencies","RefundService verifies state synchronously before issuing the refund (no eventual consistency concerns raised)"]}}
